research_topics: Adaptive IC-based signal weighting with PPO reinforcement learning
Walk-forward optimization with parameterized window lengths
Regime-adaptive weighted ensemble learning (per-regime signal weights)
Direction-specific signal gating (BUY-only / SELL-only conditional alpha)
Multi-agent bull/bear debate trading systems (TradingAgents v0.2.4)
QuantBench: standardized AI benchmark for quantitative investment
Regime-aware stock prediction with autoencoder-gated dual transformers
Boruta feature selection with CNN-LSTM for crypto prediction
Conditional asymmetric alpha generation under regime stress

findings.topic: Adaptive IC-Based Signal Weighting with PPO
findings.finding: PPO-based RL dynamically weights alpha signals using rolling IC (Spearman correlation) and IC Ratio (IC mean / IC stddev). New QuantBench benchmark (arxiv 2504.18600) reveals continual learning is critical to address distribution shifts in low signal-to-noise environments. Walk-forward IC with 3/6/12-month rolling steps measures market evolution speed. Our system's equal-weight approach leaves significant edge on the table.
findings.relevance_to_us: Direct upgrade path for signal_engine.py _weighted_consensus(). We have ic_computation.py already started. The QuantBench finding about continual learning validates our recency-weighted 70/30 approach but suggests going further: exponential decay with half-life tuned per signal volatility rather than fixed 7-day window.

findings.finding: Feb 2026 paper (arxiv 2602.10785) demonstrates that walk-forward performance is highly dependent on chosen window sizes — there is no universal optimal window. The authors parameterize training/testing window lengths as hyperparameters and optimize jointly using Robust Sharpe Ratio. A Dec 2025 framework (arxiv 2512.12924) combines hypothesis-driven signal generation with RL and strict OOS testing to prevent overfitting.
findings.relevance_to_us: Our Chronos 76%→54% crash was exactly the small-sample illusion these papers address. We should implement walk-forward validation on signal_log.db with parameterized windows per signal. Fast signals (RSI, BB) need shorter windows; slow signals (COT, econ_calendar) need longer ones.

findings.finding: April 2026 paper (arxiv 2604.27207) demonstrates regime-adaptive ensemble where a weight-learning neural network dynamically optimizes inter-model contributions across operating regimes. In financial application (arxiv 2603.04441), parametric regime models produce dramatically lower turnover and smoother weight evolution. Regime inference stability is a first-order determinant of portfolio drawdown. A March 2026 paper (arxiv 2603.19136) achieves 0.59% MAPE with autoencoder-gated dual transformers using regime-adaptive control, outperforming HMM-LSTM at 0.87%.
findings.relevance_to_us: Our current regime system uses flat penalty multipliers (0.75x ranging, 0.80x high-vol). The research shows per-regime signal weight sets are dramatically better. RSI/BB/mean_reversion excel in ranging; trend/momentum excel in trending. We could train a simple logistic classifier on [realized_vol, ADX, Hurst_exponent] to select regime-specific weight vectors from a lookup table — no neural net needed for v1.

findings.finding: Research confirms long signals are generally more accurate than short signals due to equity risk premium, but the asymmetry varies by signal type and asset class. AI-driven frameworks mapping 800+ equities into daily directional signals achieve Sharpe >2.5 by conditionally differentiating high vs low crash probability periods. Conditional alpha generation shows that buy-side and sell-side accuracy should be tracked and gated independently.
findings.relevance_to_us: CRITICAL for our system. Qwen3 SELL accuracy is 73.5% but BUY is mediocre. news_event BUY accuracy is 20.5% (anti-signal) but SELL may be fine. We should split accuracy tracking into buy_accuracy and sell_accuracy in accuracy_stats.py and gate each direction independently. A signal could be SELL-only-enabled if sell_accuracy >= 55% but buy_accuracy < 47%. This is a low-effort, high-impact change.

findings.finding: Jan 2026 paper (arxiv 2601.02878) shows hybrid LLM-Transformer reduces RMSE by 5.28% vs vanilla Transformer. LLMs serve as signal generators extracting directional sentiment + confidence scores, fused with structured price features through noise-robust gating. Sentiment-aware approach (arxiv 2508.04975) uses LLMs to generate formulaic alphas from historical features + sentiment. Key insight: LLM as analyst generating structured signals, not as direct price predictor.
findings.relevance_to_us: Validates our architecture: Qwen3 and Ministral as signal generators, not direct predictors. But we're missing the noise-robust gating mechanism — when LLM confidence is low, the signal should be automatically suppressed. Our current approach treats all LLM outputs equally. Adding a confidence threshold gate (suppress when LLM self-reported confidence < 60%) would reduce noise from uncertain LLM calls.

findings.finding: TradingAgents now at 51K+ GitHub stars. BlackRock + Columbia University (Apr 2026) published Three-Layer Multi-Agent Framework: dedicated Bull agents, Bear agents, Risk Supervisor — consistently outperforms single-model LLMs. Framework uses deep_think_llm for Bull/Bear debate agents and quick_think_llm for analyst agents. India-trade-cli (Vibe Trading) shows 7 parallel analyst agents + debate for NSE/BSE. Key: adversarial debate among LLM agents improves reasoning quality.
findings.relevance_to_us: Layer 2 is a single Claude invocation. Cheapest win: restructure the prompt to require explicit bull_case, bear_case, and risk_check JSON sections before verdict. Medium-term: spawn two Claude subprocess calls (bull advocate + bear advocate) and a third synthesizer. The BlackRock paper validates this approach at institutional scale.

findings.topic: Boruta Feature Selection for Crypto ML
findings.finding: Combining Boruta feature selection with CNN-LSTM achieves 82.44% accuracy for BTC direction prediction. 2026 ACM paper on multimodal BTC forecasting found technical indicators (MA, BB, MACD, short lags) dominated per SHAP analysis, while on-chain and sentiment contributed mainly during shocks/news. Five modalities: market data, on-chain, Fear & Greed, news, Reddit. Key finding: price-related features dominate; blockchain/sentiment features have negligible marginal contribution in normal conditions.
findings.relevance_to_us: Our on-chain signal is at 60% (45 samples) — consistent with research showing on-chain contributes mainly during regime shifts. We should weight on-chain higher during high-volatility regimes and lower during calm periods. Also validates keeping Fear & Greed as active (58.6% accuracy, 10K samples — our best-sampled high-accuracy signal).

findings.topic: Conditional Asymmetric Alpha Under Regime Stress
findings.finding: Regime-aware strategies that conditionally differentiate high vs low crash probability periods achieve asymmetric P&L: retaining gains in normal conditions while mitigating losses during stress. The ComSIA 2026 paper (arxiv 2601.19504) demonstrates a hybrid AI system integrating technical analysis, ML, and sentiment for regime-adaptive equity strategies. Daily allocation with regime inference stability as the primary determinant of drawdown control.
findings.relevance_to_us: Our drawdown circuit breaker in risk_management.py is binary (on/off). Research suggests a gradient approach: as regime stress indicators rise, progressively reduce position sizes and increase confidence thresholds for new entries. This would prevent the Bold strategy's -7.09% loss pattern (entered during F&G=5).

recommended_improvements.title: Direction-Specific Signal Gating
recommended_improvements.description: Split accuracy tracking into buy_accuracy and sell_accuracy per signal per ticker. Gate each direction independently: if sell_accuracy >= 55% but buy_accuracy < 47%, enable signal for SELL only. Immediate win for Qwen3 (73.5% SELL) and news_event (20.5% BUY = anti-signal). Requires changes to accuracy_stats.py vote counting and signal_engine.py gating logic.

recommended_improvements.title: IC-Based Signal Weighting (v1: rolling IC weights)
recommended_improvements.description: Replace equal-weight voting with rolling IC weights. Compute 30-day rolling Spearman IC per signal using signal_log.db outcomes. Weight consensus proportionally. Use exponential decay with per-signal half-life instead of fixed 7-day recency window. ic_computation.py already exists — extend it.

recommended_improvements.title: Walk-Forward Validation Framework
recommended_improvements.description: Implement parameterized walk-forward validation on signal_log.db. Per-signal window optimization: fast signals (RSI, BB) use 2-week train / 3-day test; slow signals (COT, econ) use 3-month train / 2-week test. Report walk-forward Sharpe and accuracy stability. Prevents Chronos-style small-sample illusions.

recommended_improvements.title: Regime-Specific Signal Weight Vectors
recommended_improvements.description: Replace flat regime penalties (0.75x/0.80x) with per-regime weight lookup tables. Train simple classifier on [realized_vol_20d, ADX_14, Hurst_exponent] to identify 3 regimes. Precompute optimal signal weights per regime from historical signal_log.db data. No neural net needed for v1.

recommended_improvements.title: LLM Confidence Gate
recommended_improvements.description: Add confidence threshold gate for Qwen3 and Ministral signals. When LLM self-reported confidence < 60%, suppress the signal to HOLD. Aligns with noise-robust gating mechanism from hybrid LLM-Transformer research. Simple string parsing of existing LLM output.

recommended_improvements.title: Structured Bull/Bear Layer 2 Prompt
recommended_improvements.description: Restructure Layer 2 prompt to require explicit JSON sections: bull_case (3 reasons), bear_case (3 reasons), risk_assessment, verdict. This is the cheapest approximation of multi-agent debate. Proven by BlackRock+Columbia Apr 2026 research.
